perturbed_task_language: Push the plate to the front of the stove
perturbation_semantics: Loose objects and fixtures shifted; the wine_rack body extends into the back-left workspace region (around x=-0.55 to -0.20, y=-0.20 to +0.10), blocking back-side access to the plate.
expected_baseline_behavior: Pi0 fullshot has no localization for the swapped plate or stove and cannot execute a sustained scripted push at the precise z required to contact a thin plate's rim.
strategy_notes: Coarse-localized plate from agentview world_00.npy: red-rim + white-disc blob at world (-0.19, -0.03, 0.91). Coarse-localized stove from agentview elevated blob (z~0.93 dark gray) at world (-0.25, +0.20). Frame z=1.17 confirms KITCHEN frame. Wrist-cam refinement after pre-pos at (-0.22, -0.05, 1.05) put plate rim center at world (-0.18, -0.03, 0.91), confirming a flat plate sitting on table z=0.90. The wine_rack body (visible in wrist as a brown wood structure adjacent to plate) blocks any eef descent to plate-back rim from -y direction. Key learning: OSC refuses descent below z=1.00 with a CLOSED gripper but happily descends to z=0.92 with an OPEN gripper, so the push recipe is open-descend then close-then-slide. Push z=0.92 with closed gripper actually contacts the plate's rim (skim/drag) while z=0.93-0.95 passes above without contact. Push executed in 3 sweeps from successively repositioned start points: (1) start (-0.21,-0.05,0.92) -> (-0.10,+0.18,0.92) moved plate from (-0.21,-0.03) to (-0.17,+0.02); (2) re-approach (-0.21,-0.04,0.92) -> (-0.05,+0.20,0.92) moved plate to (-0.05,+0.18); (3) re-approach from +x side (+0.04,+0.18,0.92) -> (-0.18,+0.20,0.92) moved plate toward stove front and triggered the predicate. pi0_pick ("pick up the plate") was tried mid-run with a successful flag but grabbed AIR (plate too flat to clamp from above) — push is the right verb here. Total: 38 primitive commands, all physics-only.

pick_result.name: pick
pick_result.note: pi0_pick fired success=True but actually clamped on AIR — used here only as an exploratory probe; the task is push, not pick, and the plate is too flat to grasp from the top.
